summary:
The diagnosis of severe preeclampsia requires a urine protein excretion greater than 300 mg per day according to multiple medical authorities. This criterion helps differentiate levels of disease severity for better patient care management.
To summarize Agent1’s reasoning into two concise sentences focusing only on the key clinical point:

Severe preeclampsia is diagnosed when urinary protein exceeds 300 mg/day based on established medical standards. This threshold aids in differentiating disease severity for optimal treatment decisions. 

This summary captures the essential information while adhering strictly to your instructions regarding length and focus.
